subtasks.description: Scan IAM policies to extract principal, resource, action, effect, and condition attributes. Build complete inventory of active IAM policies with their configurations.
Query CloudTrail logs to identify all API calls, their principals, actions, resources, timestamps, and response statuses. Generate audit trail covering identity, action, and resource access patterns.

Cross-reference IAM policy definitions against CloudTrail activity logs. Identify policy violations, overpermissioned actions, unauthorized access attempts, and deviations from least-privilege principle. Compare actual vs. allowed actions.
Generate comprehensive compliance report including policy inventory summary, violation findings, risk severity classifications, remediation recommendations, and executive summary of security posture.

plan.cost_rationale: Four subtasks justified as minimum decomposition: (1) IAM policy extraction requires structured field extraction (extract, mid-tier for complex policy syntax and multi-field conditions); (2) CloudTrail querying is single SQL gen task (mid-tier for multi-table joins across log events); (3) Violation detection requires cross-referencing policy definitions against audit logs—two distinct sources requiring reconciliation with classification logic (cross_recon, frontier-tier for multi-signal analysis and anomaly detection); (4) Compliance report generation depends on all prior outputs and includes synthesis, risk classification, and recommendations (cross_recon, mid-tier since violation analysis is already complete upstream). No policy_action subtask added—compliance checking is integrated into violation detection and report generation as part of cross_recon reconciliation workflow. Tier assignments: IAM extraction (mid) due to schema complexity; CloudTrail (mid) due to multi-table joins; violation check (frontier) due to high-complexity cross-source matching and multi-signal anomaly detection; report (mid) since heavy lifting is upstream.

output.sql: SELECT log_id, user_id, permission, resource, access_time, action, result, DATE(access_time) AS access_date, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM access_time) AS access_hour, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS total_actions_by_user, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id, action) AS action_count_by_user, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY resource) AS total_resource_accesses, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id, result) AS result_count_by_user, CASE WHEN result = 'DENIED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_denied, COUNT(CASE WHEN result = 'DENIED' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) AS denied_count_by_user, COUNT(CASE WHEN result = 'DENIED' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY resource) AS denied_count_by_resource, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN result = 'DENIED' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id), 0), 2) AS denial_rate_pct_by_user, CASE WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN result = 'DENIED' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) > 10 THEN 'HIGH_RISK' WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN result = 'DENIED' THEN 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id) BETWEEN 5 AND 10 THEN 'MEDIUM_RISK' ELSE 'LOW_RISK' END AS user_risk_level, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY access_time DESC) AS recency_rank FROM access_log_facts ORDER BY user_id, access_time DESC
